response: The EU's GDPR, in force since May 2018, requires any organization handling EU residents' data to uphold rights like access, erasure, and explicit consent, with mandatory breach reporting within 72 hours and fines reaching up to €20 million or 4% of global annual revenue. Major penalties have hit tech giants—Meta was fined €1.2 billion in 2023, Amazon €746 million, and Google over €200 million—while the law has inspired similar privacy frameworks worldwide. Critics argue it imposes heavy compliance burdens on small businesses, creates user frustration with cookie banners, and suffers from inconsistent enforcement across member states. Despite these issues, GDPR cemented data privacy as a fundamental right and became the global benchmark for data protection legislation.
